prevalence.source: SBEM guideline context and thyroid ultrasound epidemiology
prevalence.trend: increasing detection due to ultrasound use

incidence.source: Thyroid imaging and outpatient cohort syntheses

burden.dalyPerCapita: low; burden driven by anxiety, diagnostic work-up, and overdiagnosis

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: TSH and thyroid ultrasound; risk stratification by ultrasound features; fine-needle aspiration for suspicious nodules (Bethesda system)

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no routine screening; incidental detection common
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High for benign nodules; appropriate surveillance reduces unnecessary surgery

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: SBEM-aligned ultrasound risk stratification and FNA when indicated
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Observation for low-risk nodules; FNA for suspicious nodules; surgery for malignant/high-risk cases; levothyroxine suppression rarely indicated
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Overdiagnosis and uneven access to ultrasound/FNA in smaller municipalities
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Clear guideline pathways and access to endocrinology in major centers
